jobs.company: Department of Health and Human Services
jobs.title: Toxicologist
jobs.description: Degree in toxicology or related biological/medical/veterinary science with specified coursework; 1 year specialized experience equivalent to GS-12 reviewing and interpreting toxicological studies and regulatory submissions; transcripts required.

jobs.title: Toxicologist 2
jobs.description: Nine years of experience and/or education in toxicology or related science; ability to perform risk assessment, interpret environmental data, apply state and federal regulations, communicate technical findings, and plan/prioritize complex work.

jobs.title: Toxicologist - Forensic Expert
jobs.description: Ph.D. in toxicology, 10+ years toxicology experience, board certification (ABFT or DABT) preferred, membership in toxicology organization, ~20% travel, clean background check and valid driver's license required.

jobs.title: Senior Scientist, Study Toxicologist
jobs.description: PhD/MS with 3 years toxicology experience or BS with 5 years; experience overseeing safety pharmacology and toxicology studies, GLP knowledge, CRO study management, SEND datasets, and regulatory submission support; occasional travel (~20%).

jobs.title: Pharmacologist/Physiologist/Toxicologist
jobs.description: U.S. citizen required; background investigation and Public Trust. Mandatory degree in relevant biological/medical/veterinary/physical science or pharmacy with specified coursework. Minimum 52 weeks specialized experience in toxicology, pharmacology, or physiology; ability to perform hazard analyses and prepare technical reports.

jobs.title: Public Health Toxicologist (Ph.D.)
jobs.description: Ph.D. in toxicology/public health or related field, strong human health toxicology and risk assessment expertise, excellent writing and communication, ability to manage multiple projects and work independently or in teams; must be authorized to work in the U.S.

jobs.title: Chemist/Toxicologist (Part Time/Limited Duration)
jobs.location: Yonkers or Washington or Colchester or Arizona or California or Connecticut or Illinois or Maryland or Massachusetts or Michigan or New Hampshire or New Jersey or New York or Texas or Vermont or Virginia or District of Columbia
jobs.description: 5+ years in analytical chemistry/toxicology or risk assessment; BS in chemistry/toxicology/chemical engineering preferred; familiarity with ICP-MS, GC-MS, LC-MS/MS; regulatory knowledge (FDA, EPA, CPSC, REACH, Health Canada); strong communication.

jobs.title: Research Fellow, Medical Device Toxicologist
jobs.description: Ph.D. in Toxicology or related field, 12+ years medical device industry experience, team management experience, DABT preferred, ophthalmic device experience a plus.

jobs.company: Bechtel: Engineering, Construction, Procurement & Project Management
jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Bachelor's in environmental science or related field, 4+ years environmental experience, valid driver’s license, skills in permitting, monitoring, sampling, data analysis, and environmental impact assessment.

jobs.title: Environmental Health Scientist
jobs.description: Bachelor of Science in a scientific field; Utah Environmental Health Scientist License or ability to obtain within 1 year.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Bachelor's in environmental engineering, environmental science or geology; 0–2 years' related experience; experience with data collection, sampling/testing, HSE practices, document preparation, and project support.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Bachelor's in environmental engineering/science with 3+ years federal environmental compliance; knowledge of Clean Air and Clean Water Acts; MS Office; DoD background check; US citizenship; able to travel up to 30%.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist I
jobs.description: Entry-level environmental scientist; field data collection, data processing, quality control, outdoor work; GIS/GPS experience; weekend/holiday work.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Bachelor's in environmental science, chemistry, or biology; valid driver’s license; CPR/First Aid; ability to conduct field work and data reporting.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Bachelor's degree and 3 years relevant experience; environmental compliance, permitting, sampling, data analysis, report preparation, regulatory agency coordination, and valid driver's license required.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: 5+ years experience in waste approvals, strong knowledge of RCRA and DOT, Bachelor's in Chemistry or Environmental Science (or equivalent), advanced Microsoft Excel, database/SQL familiarity, technical writing, and occasional travel.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Bachelor's degree in geology/engineering/environmental science, 0–3+ years' relevant experience, field sampling and remediation support, proficiency with Microsoft Office, valid driver's license, ability to travel and perform outdoor fieldwork.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.location: Omaha or Lincoln or Sioux Falls
jobs.description: Bachelor's in environmental or related natural science, strong written/verbal skills, valid driver's license; 2 years' experience preferred. Ability to perform site visits, field data collection, regulatory reviews, and technical reporting.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Requires a four-year degree in environmental science/biology/ecology/conservation, professional ecological monitoring experience, willingness for rugged fieldwork, valid driver's license, native plant ID preferred, and strong technical, quantitative, and writing skills.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Bachelor's in geology or environmental science required; entry-level, primarily field work (95–100%); valid clean driver\u0000s license; pass background check and drug test; 40-hour HAZWOPER preferred; Microsoft Office proficiency.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist II
jobs.location: Seattle or Everett or Olympia or Bellevue or Gig Harbor
jobs.description: Bachelor's in Environmental Science, 5+ years technical/field experience preparing environmental documents, ability to lead wetland/stream delineations and habitat surveys, prepare environmental permitting documentation, strong writing and MS Office skills.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Bachelor's in Geology or Environmental Science required. Primarily field work (95–100%), experience with remediation sampling, H&S focus, valid clean driver’s license, pass background and drug tests; 40-hour HAZWOPER preferred; MS Office proficiency.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist Journeyman
jobs.description: Bachelor’s in engineering or science; 3-10 years in environmental restoration; U.S. citizenship and Secret clearance; contract experience; MS Office proficiency.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist III
jobs.description: Bachelor's in a natural/biological/physical science (or equivalent) and three years' experience in natural resources or environmental control; ability to perform fieldwork and statewide travel.

jobs.title: MS4 Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Bachelor’s degree in environmental science or related field; 2-4 years experience; ArcGIS; strong written and verbal communication; able to work independently and in a team.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist II/III
jobs.description: Bachelor’s in environmental science or related field; 3–5 years environmental consulting; strong writing; travel ability; familiarity with regulations.

jobs.title: Senior Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Conduct environmental investigations, wetland impact analyses and permitting, ecological assessment/restoration, field data collection and sampling, GIS/GPS mapping, technical report writing, and project management/business development support.

jobs.title: Senior Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Bachelor's degree required, 10–15 years environmental consulting experience, proven business development (doer-seller), project management, regulatory knowledge, excellent communication, and ability to lead technical work and mentor staff.

jobs.title: Senior Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Conduct environmental investigations, wetland impact analyses and permitting, ecological assessment and restoration support, field data collection and sampling, GPS/GIS mapping, technical report writing, NEPA support, and project management/business development.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist I
jobs.description: Bachelor's in geology or related field required; valid driver's license, frequent daily travel and occasional overnight travel; knowledge of hydrology, hydrogeology, GIS, Microsoft Office, and environmental laws; ability to review permits, analyze data, and communicate technical findings.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist III
jobs.location: Tampa or Florida or United States
jobs.description: 5+ years environmental consulting experience, Bachelor’s degree in a related field, PE or PG required/obtainable, HAZWOPER 40-hour preferred (training provided if needed), valid driver’s license, ability to travel regionally, strong communication and data skills, MS Office proficiency.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist II - Williamsport
jobs.description: 5+ years environmental experience, B.S. in Environmental Science/Ecology/Biology, independent wetland/stream delineation, PADEP/USACE permit preparation experience, sub‑meter GPS use, valid driver’s license, MS Office proficiency.

jobs.title: Senior Environmental Scientist
jobs.location: Colorado Springs or Denver or Fayetteville or Lincoln or Little Rock or Loveland or Oklahoma City or Omaha or Overland Park or Remote
jobs.description: Bachelor’s degree in environmental science, urban planning, or biological/scientific discipline; 10 years environmental experience; 5 years transportation project experience; strong communication and project management skills.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist I/II
jobs.location: Springfield or Chicago or United States
jobs.description: Bachelor's in environmental science or related field required; 1+ years professional experience preferred. Experience with environmental monitoring, scientific instrumentation, telemetry, GIS (ESRI ArcPro), Microsoft Office/Excel, data management, and valid driver's license required.

jobs.title: Risk Assessment Lead - Cybersecurity Risk Oversight
jobs.location: Minneapolis or Cincinnati or Charlotte or Tempe or Saint Louis or Irving
jobs.description: Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience, typically 10+ years in technology/cybersecurity risk; strong knowledge of information security domains and regulatory frameworks; ability to perform independent risk assessments and influence stakeholders.

jobs.title: Lead Risk Assessment Research Scientist
jobs.description: Lead risk assessment researcher with toxicology & risk assessment expertise; develops human health risk assessments and communicates findings; strong leadership and collaboration.

jobs.title: Compliance Risk Assessment Manager
jobs.location: Charlotte or New York or Greenville or Mount Laurel or Portland
jobs.description: Undergraduate degree or equivalent experience and 7+ years in regulatory/compliance/risk management; familiarity with compliance risk assessment, US banking regulations, strong analytical and communication skills, and advanced desktop tool proficiency.

jobs.title: Director of Risk Assessment
jobs.description: RN or Licensed Nurse with 5+ years in long-term care; nursing administration in California; risk assessment and regulatory compliance; willingness to travel across California.

jobs.title: Compliance Risk Assessment Contractor
jobs.location: New York City, New York, United States
jobs.description: Bachelor's degree required, 3+ years regulatory/compliance experience in financial services, experience writing compliance risk assessments, strong communication and organisational skills, Microsoft Office and GRC tool familiarity, interest in ICA/CAMS.

jobs.title: Risk Assessment Associate (Youth AI Safety Institute)
jobs.description: Bachelor's degree; 2+ years in research/tech; analytical, strong communication; technical aptitude with AI tools; ability to handle sensitive content.

jobs.title: Senior Enterprise Risk Analyst, Risk Assessment
jobs.description: 6+ years financial risk management experience, 3+ years with SAS/R/Python/SQL/Matlab, Bachelor's required (relevant fields), FRM/CFA preferred, strong quantitative, analytical, and communication skills.

jobs.title: Enterprise Compliance and Operational Risk Analyst - Risk Assessment
jobs.description: Bachelor's + 6 years in risk/audit or HS/GED + 7 years; experience in risk assessments, testing, reporting, GRC tools, data analysis, and strong communication; Microsoft Office proficiency.

jobs.title: Vice President - RFP Risk Assessment Manager - Payments
jobs.description: Experience with TS or Commercial Card services, strong proposal/technical/business writing, intermediate-advanced Microsoft Word and Adobe Professional, detail-oriented, able to multi-task and work varying hours to meet deadlines.

jobs.title: GRC-Risk Assessment
jobs.description: 9+ years experience in business operations or similar; advanced Microsoft Excel, Microsoft SharePoint, and Jira skills; hands-on risk assessment experience; strong written and verbal communication; hybrid day-shift work experience.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Bachelor's in a related field, minimum 5 years water quality experience with surface/groundwater sampling, Hawaii-specific knowledge, field sampling and instrumentation experience, MS Office proficiency, valid driver’s license, ability to perform fieldwork in remote/storm conditions; occasional evenings/weekends.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist Manager
jobs.description: BS in environmental science/engineering, 10+ years' professional experience, knowledge of stormwater permitting and water quality monitoring, CPSWQ/CPESC and QSD/QSP/QISP certifications preferred, California driver's license, proficiency with Microsoft Office.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist I
jobs.description: Bachelor's in natural science required; some field or water-quality monitoring experience desired. Must be able to swim, hold a valid Texas driver\u0019s license, complete Texas Boater Education within 6 months, and have skills in field data collection, QA/QC, GIS, and data analysis.

jobs.title: Environmental Health Scientist I - Environmental Safety
jobs.location: Lincoln or Lincoln County or Dawson County or Frontier County or Red Willow County or Buffalo County or Perkins County or Chase County or Hayes County or Dundy County or Hitchcock County or Gosper County or Furnas County
jobs.description: Post-high-school coursework in physical/life sciences or environmental health; valid driver's license or independent transportation; ability to travel for fieldwork (≈80%), walk uneven terrain, lift 20 lbs, climb ladders, and work outdoors.

jobs.title: Lead Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Bachelor's in environmental or related life science, 7+ years environmental science experience, knowledge of environmental laws and compliance, leadership and communication skills, proficiency with office technology, occasional travel.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist (Health Physicist)
jobs.location: Salt Lake City, Utah, United States
jobs.description: Working knowledge of nuclear physics, radioactive materials handling, internal dosimetry, radiation detection and calibration, dose and shielding calculations, knowledge of Utah Radiation Control rules and US NRC regs, and strong oral/written communication skills.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist 1
jobs.description: Bachelor's in environmental science or related field required. Experience with environmental sampling, regulatory compliance, inspections, data management, Microsoft Office and ArcGIS. Valid CA driver's license and ability to lift 50 lbs.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description: Bachelor’s degree and 5+ years of experience; wetland surveys and permitting; field and project coordination.

jobs.title: Environmental Chemist/Scientist
jobs.description: Chemist with 3-5 years of experience in reviewing and validating environmental data, managing a small wet laboratory, and supporting field programs.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist Manager
jobs.description:
Are you driven to grow, lead, and make a meaningful impact? At Apex, we're building more than a consulting and engineering firm—we're creating a place where your career accelerates, your contributions matter, and your potential is fully realized. We believe your growth is our growth, which is why we invest in your development at every stage of your career. Here, you'll work on projects that shape communities, protect the environment, and create lasting impact, all while being empowered with the autonomy and flexibility to do your best work.  

  

Fueled by high quality delivery, exceptional client retention, and strategic acquisitions, Apex Companies continues to rank among the fastest\-growing firms in the AEC industry, recently recognized by the Zweig Group for our industry\-leading growth. Our success is grounded in strong leadership, a collaborative culture, and a shared commitment to delivering exceptional outcomes.  

  

As we continue to grow, we're focused on building a team of high\-performing professionals who are ready to lead, collaborate, and push what's possible. At Apex, you don't just join a company, you help shape what comes next.

  

CWE, An Apex Company, is a dynamic, award\-winning provider of consulting, engineering, technical, and construction management services. Clients trust CWE to provide innovative and sustainable solutions to solve complex problems involving civil infrastructure, water resources, and the environment. CWE is an integrated firm with a reputation for delivering engineering solutions that improve the quality of life in a fiscally responsible and holistic manner, consistent with our vision of Creating a better tomorrow, today®.



We develop and implement real world solutions that are practical and cost\-effective to create value for our clients and enhance our communities and environment.


**Why Work for Us?**



This is not a "job" – it is a career with a rapidly\-growing organization in a dynamic industry that recognizes excellent performance and provides opportunities for growth and advancement. This is a fantastic opportunity to develop valuable engineering and technical skills under industry recognized leaders. In addition, as a firm that is leading the industry with innovative and creative approaches to solving environmental and engineering challenges, our employees leave the office each day knowing they have contributed to a positive movement that will improve our communities, environment, and the world.



We have an exciting opportunity for an outstanding individual to join our team as an **Environmental Scientist Manager** at our headquarters in **Fullerton, California.**


**General Characteristics**


* Responsible for managing municipal, utility, industrial, and commercials clients with stormwater planning and compliance efforts associated with Phase I and II Municipal Separate Storm Sewer System (MS4\) Permits, the Construction General Permit (CGP), and Industrial General Permit (IGP).
* Manage and Interpret complex regulatory permits, assist clients with regulatory compliance, prepare technical reports, conduct environmental compliance inspections and sampling, and complete high quality deliverables on time and within budget
* Lead Manage environmental sampling, testing, and observations (primarily surface water, stormwater, groundwater, wastewater


**Critical Tasks and Responsibilities**


* Project Management
* Interpret regulatory permits and formulate compliance strategies
* Prepare comprehensive approaches, work plans, technical reports and documents (watershed management plans, stormwater management plans, annual reports, non\-stormwater discharge source assessments, adaptive management plans, compliance guidance documents, compliance reports, rain and sampling event summary reports, SWPPPs, WQMPs, etc.)
* Execute/manage field investigations, including mobilization/demobilization decisions
* Manage workload of other Environmental Scientist staff
* Prepare/Monitor Project Budgets
* Meet with clients regarding work and performance of team, and regarding current and future work
* Perform construction site and facility environmental compliance inspections (QSP, QISP, FOG)
* Conduct environmental sampling, testing, and observations (primarily surface water, stormwater, groundwater, wastewater, along with soils and sediments)
* Make field observations and perform data measurement and collection assignments
* Data management and analysis (compilation, manipulation, and evaluation of data)
* Develop and implement monitoring and reporting plans and field studies
* Perform regulatory and water quality data analyses
* Participate/lead in technical presentations


**Required Competencies and Qualifications**


* BS in Environmental Science, Civil or Environmental Engineering, or a related field
* 10\+ years of professional work experience
* Knowledge of stormwater permitting and planning; water quality monitoring; BMPs; and environmental compliance inspections
* CPSWQ/CPESC certification
* QSD or QSP and QISP certifications
* Able to read, interpret, and analyze plans, technical manuals, and permits
* Strong analytical skills
* Responsible and Accountable for project delivery
* Perform a high level of QA/QC on work products
* Ability to pay attention to detail with a high level of accuracy
* Demonstrates a strong work ethic
* Excellent writer and communicator
* Proactive approach/self\-starter/takes initiative/positive attitude/team player
* Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite
* Ability to kneel, climb, and lift up to 50lbs
* Valid California driver's license and insurable driving record


**Desired Competencies and Qualifications**


**The following competencies and qualifications are a plus, but not required:**


* MS or PhD in Environmental Science, Civil or Environmental Engineering, or a related field
* Proficient with ArcGIS
* OSHA 40\-hour HAZWOPER training
* Perform maintenance and calibration of environmental monitoring ins

In addition, as a firm that is leading the industry with innovative and creative approaches to solving environmental and engineering challenges, our employees leave the office each day knowing they have contributed to a positive movement that will improve our communities, environment, and the world.



We have an exciting opportunity for an outstanding individual to join our team as a **Environmental Scientist I** at our headquarters in **Fullerton, California.**


**General Characteristics**


* Responsible for assisting municipal, utility, industrial, and commercials clients with stormwater planning and compliance efforts associated with Phase I and II Municipal Separate Storm Sewer System (MS4\) Permits, the Construction General Permit (CGP), and Industrial General Permit (IGP).
* Interpret complex regulatory permits, assist clients with regulatory compliance, prepare technical reports, conduct environmental compliance inspections and sampling, and complete high quality deliverables on time and within budget
* Assist environmental sampling, testing, and observations (primarily surface water, stormwater, groundwater, wastewater


**Critical Tasks and Responsibilities**


* Interpret regulatory permits and formulate compliance strategies
* Prepare comprehensive technical reports and documents (watershed management plans, stormwater management plans, annual reports, non\-stormwater discharge source assessments, adaptive management plans, compliance guidance documents, compliance reports, rain and sampling event summary reports, SWPPPs, WQMPs, etc.)
* Perform construction site and facility environmental compliance inspections (QSP, QISP, FOG)
* Conduct environmental sampling, testing, and observations (primarily surface water, stormwater, groundwater, wastewater, along with soils and sediments)
* Make field observations and perform data measurement and collection assignments
* Data management and analysis (compilation, manipulation, and evaluation of data)
* Perform maintenance and calibration of environmental monitoring instruments, meters, probes, and automated sampling equipment
* Develop and implement monitoring and reporting plans and field studies
* Perform regulatory and water quality data analyses


**Required Competencies and Qualifications**


* BS degree in Environmental Science, Civil or Environmental Engineering, or a related field.
* Familiarity with spreadsheets and data management (i.e. Microsoft Excel, etc.)
* Abilities in Microsoft Office software (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Project, etc.).
* Abilities using ESRI ArcMap/ArcGIS Pro/ArcGIS Online software
* Ability to grasp and become proficient in new software and technologies
* Able to read, interpret, and analyze plans, technical manuals, and permits
* Ability to pay attention to detail with a high level of accuracy.
* Good interpersonal abilities for working in a team environment.
* Excellent written and oral communication skills.
* Ability to multi\-task, prioritize, and take direction.
* Strong analytical and problem solving skills.
* Ability to self\-motivate and work productively without supervision.
* Proactive approach/self\-starter/takes initiative
* Positive attitude/team player
* Ability to kneel, climb, and lift up to 50 lbs.
* Valid California driver's license and insurable driving record


**Desired Competencies and Qualifications**


* MS, PhD or other advanced degree(s) in related fields.
* Envision Sustainability Professional (ENV SP)
* CPSWQ/CPESC certification
* QSD or QSP and QISP certifications
* Knowledge of stormwater permitting and planning; water quality monitoring; BMPs; and environmental compliance inspections
* Member of professional practice organization (e.g. ASCE, ACEC, APWA, etc..)
* Involved in outreach and service activitie

jobs.title: Environmental Specialist
jobs.description:
Job Summary


The Environmental Specialist is responsible for inspecting, sampling, and monitoring of environmental activities, and ensuring conformance with Federal, Tribal, and other related regulations and ordinance. In addition, provides general administrative support duties for assigned department or functional area. The duties include taking meeting minutes, writing correspondences, preparing quarterly reports, managing files, and performing general office tasks.
Job Description


• Monitors and maintains environmental protection and monitoring systems including but not limited to soil, groundwater, surface water and air monitoring systems as required. • Inspects and investigates sources of pollution to protect the public and environment and ensures conformance with Federal, Tribal and other related regulations and ordinances. • Operates, maintains, and performs minor repair of environmental monitoring and testing devices. • Collects environmental samples for compliance. • Compiles, evaluates, and analyzes data as required. • Assists in preparing reports to Federal and State agencies regarding specific environmental monitoring program. • Conducts and participates in community presentations, forum, and education activities. • Maintains all related files in a neat and orderly condition at all times. • Ensures files contain all pertinent documentation and information, including support documentation, correspondence, fiscal budgets and notifications. • Ensures confidentiality of all files and information concerning environmental activities, including the contents of grants, and any issues involving violations, mitigation and remediation in accordance with the policies governing confidentiality. • Performs a variety of clerical duties, such as composing and preparing documents, correspondence and quarterly reports, creating forms, faxing, copying, and filing. • Takes meeting minutes and maintains attendance logs, notifies appropriate stakeholders of upcoming meetings through mail, e\-mail, and telephone. • Screens incoming phone calls and routes to appropriate team member. • Prepares check requests and tracks expenses. • Complies with and follows departmental policies and standard operating procedures, and any applicable governmental laws and regulations. • Ensures that a clean, safe, hazard\-free work environment is maintained. • Maintains the highest level of confidentiality, impartiality, and reliability. • Performs all other duties as assigned. • Does not provide supervision to others. • High school diploma or General education degree (GED) required. • Associate degree or greater in Environmental Science • Possession of a valid California Class C Driver’s License and a satisfactory driving record. • 2\+ years of administrative and/or related field experience preferred • A four\-year degree in biology or a related field can be substituted for experience. • 36 units of science course work and 6 months to 1 year administrative and/or related field experience upon review of course work. Please submit transcripts along with resume. • Knowledge of methods, materials, and equipment used in the operation and maintenance of environmental monitoring system. • Knowledge of environmental issues, laws, and regulations applicable to compliance and protection. • Knowledge of definitions and classifications of hazardous waste and materials plus their handling and tracking. • Knowledge of principles of business letter writing, report preparation, technical research and method of data analysis. • Knowledge of Microsoft Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint, and other computer programs. • Detail\-oriented, organized, self\-motivated, and values teamwork. • Good mathematical skills (i.e. calculate volume, pressure, quantities and qualities). • Ability to operate a personal computer and safely operate a vehicle. • Ability to communicate clearly and concisely, both orally and in writing. • Ability to prepare and conduct presentations to individuals and large groups. • Ability to exercise sound judgment in making recommendations. • Ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with those contacted in the course of work. • Ability to work independently and maintain a high degree of confidentiality. • Ability to review official documents such as grants, regulations, technical specifications, environmental documents, ordinance and statues, P\&P’s, and legal terminology. • Ability to multitask and work in a fast\-paced, deadline\-oriented environment.**Hiring Preference**


The Viejas Band of Kumeyaay Indians is an equal opportunity employer. Consistent with tribal and federal law, the Viejas Band of Kumeyaay Indians applies Native American preference in hiring.

jobs.title: Environmental Compliance Specialist
jobs.description:
**JOB TITLE:** Environmental Compliance Specialist**LOCATION:** Santa Barbara**FLSA EMPLOYMENT CLASSIFICATION:** Exempt**REPORTS TO:** Risk Manager **POSITION SUMMARY:** The Environmental Specialist is responsible for ensuring the workplace complies with all applicable EHS regulations by conducting audits, developing safety plans, training employees on proper procedures, and actively identifying and mitigating potential hazards to protect employees’ health and the environment. The individual will ensure the organization complies with all local, state, and federal regulations while fostering a culture of continuous improvement in Environmental Health and Safety (EHS). **DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES** include the following.  

* Partner with the Risk \& Safety Manager to promote a culture of safety and environmental stewardship across all departments in alignment with the MarBorg standard by conducting routine safety and environmental inspections for all locations to identify potential hazards and ensure compliance.
* Serve as a key EHS resource for management and employees by driving safety and environmental initiatives, conducting inspections in all work areas, identifying potential hazards, and fostering a proactive culture of risk prevention and regulatory compliance.
* Oversee environmental, health, and safety compliance activities, including exposure assessments, routine inspections, hazard recognition, incident management, risk evaluation, and implementation of corrective actions.
* Act as primary contact for all regulatory inspections and maintain all necessary permits and documentation for safety and operational compliance.
* Work in collaboration with the Risk \& Safety Manager on investigations of workplace accidents and near misses, determining root causes, recommending corrective actions, and behavior training solutions.
* Prepare and report weekly to Senior Management, highlighting environmental compliance, audit findings, operational risks, and progress on open items and opportunities for continuous improvement.
* Makes recommendations for improvements for overall safety \& training, which will mitigate overall risk.
* Collaborate with the Safety Manager to support DOT compliance for drivers, including training records and driver qualification files, with all applicable DOT/FMCSA regulations, including the FMCSA Clearinghouse.
* Assist with the administration of a DOT\-compliant random drug and alcohol testing program.
* Maintain and ensure all required documentation, reports, and records related to compliance are accurate and completed in accordance with company and regulatory requirements.
* Maintain and ensure compliance with all relevant local, state, and federal environmental regulations and permit requirements.
* Demonstrate leadership by creating an environment that fosters teamwork, values diversity, supports and respects all team and company staff members, internal and external customers, and vendors.
* Maintains a feeling of pride in work; strives to achieve all goals.
* Supports the MarBorg Mission.
* Perform other duties as assigned.

 **SUPERVISORY RESPONSIBILITIES**
None **QUALIFICATIONS:**
To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. **KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, ABILITIES:**  

* Minimum of 4 years’ experience in an environmental, health and safety role in a related industry. Knowledge of the waste industry preferred.
* Working knowledge of EHS regulations and compliance
* Knowledge of the waste industry.
* Ability to conduct risk assessments and hazard analysis.
* Ability to interact with regulatory agencies.
* Solid understanding of federal, state, and local laws, including regulations from DOT, RCRA, SPCC, SWPPP, SMARTS, CUPA, CERS, and OSHA.
* Familiarity with investigating incidents and using root cause analysis techniques.
* Strong interpersonal, communication, and leadership skills.
* Ability to actively listen, build and maintain effective working relationships with internal/external colleagues and stakeholders.
* Ability to prioritize and successfully manage projects, programs, and timelines.
* Problem\-solving and analytical skills.
* Ability to travel to designated facilities as needed to perform the job.
* Ability to maintain a high level of confidentiality.
* Demonstrate attention to detail, organization, and deliverables, including driving projects to completion in accordance with established timelines.
* Bilingual in English and Spanish.
* Proficient with the Microsoft Office suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and Outlook)

 **EDUCATION and/or EXPERIENCE*** Bachelor’s degree (B.A.) in Environmental Science, Occupational Safety, or a related field.
* Minimum of 4 years or more in an environmental, health, and safety role in a related industry.
* Comprehensive understanding of OSHA, EPA, RCRA, SPCC, SWPPP, SMARTS, CUPA, CERS, and other relevant regulations.

 **CORE COMPETENCIES:*** Doing Good – (i.e. Code of Conduct).
* Making Good – (i.e. Accountability, responsibility, and ownership for a problem).
* Treating People Well – (i.e. Works cooperatively and effectively with others).
* Environmentally Focused (i.e. Employee takes initiative as a LEED building initiative to preserve and improve our local environment for future generations).
* Observant: (i.e. Employee can identify safety or operational deficiencies and quickly implement a correction).
* Safety Matters: Employee actively participates and promotes a safe and healthy workplace.

 **JOB SPECIFIC COMPETENCIES:**
Adaptability, Analytical, Attention to Detail, Communication, Leadership, Problem Solving **LANGUAGE SKILLS**
Ability to read and interpret documents such as safety rules, operating and maintenance instructions, and procedu

jobs.title: Senior Environmental Scientist
jobs.description:
AI Job Summary
* 5\+ years of relevant experience in environmental consulting or a related field.
* Experience preparing Environmental Impact Statements and environmental authority applications/approvals.
* Full Australian working rights (mandatory).

Sign up or log in to analyse my fit


### **Role Type**


Permanent • Full\-time • Mid\-level Senior
### **Description**


**About the role**


We are seeking a **Senior Environmental Scientist** to join our Brisbane team. In this role, you will lead and contribute to a diverse range of environmental projects, applying your technical expertise and leadership to deliver high\-quality outcomes for our clients. You’ll play a key role in shaping project delivery, mentoring junior staff, and strengthening our presence in the region.


**What you’ll do:**


* Lead and contribute to the preparation of Environmental Impact Statements, environmental authority applications, and other environmental approvals.
* Provide expert environmental consulting advice to clients across various sectors.
* Write and review technical reports to ensure clarity, accuracy, and compliance.
* Develop fee proposals and manage projects to meet deadlines and budgets.
* Liaise with government agencies on behalf of AARC and our clients.
* Manage and review work from specialised sub\-consultants.
* Mentor and support the training and development of junior team members.
* Contribute to business development initiatives and foster a positive team culture.
* Undertake site visits across Australasia and beyond, as required.
* Represent AARC’s values and culture in all professional interactions.
  

**To be successful in this role, you will need:**


* A tertiary qualification in Environmental Science, Ecology, or a related discipline
* 5 years of relevant experience in environmental consulting or a related field
* Strong project management capabilities, with a demonstrated ability to lead and deliver projects within time and budget constraints
* Full Australian working rights (mandatory)
* A valid Australian driver’s licence
* A collaborative, proactive approach with a strong commitment to quality and client service.
  

**About Us**


APEM Group is leading global environment consultancy providing expert advice to clients in a wide range of industries including water, renewables, infrastructure, marine and ports, transport, construction, and development. Following significant growth journey with operations based in the UK, EU and USA, APEM Group strategically expanded into Australia in early 2024\. Our presence in the Australian market will further enhance the aim of expanding on our consultancy services in both the Australian market and wider Asia Pacific region as part of the next stage of our Global growth journey.


As part of the APEM Group, AARC Environmental Solutions provides expert environmental solutions to the resource, critical minerals, and development sectors. Established in Queensland in 1996, AARC has a proven track record of delivering innovative, data and science\-led insights to clients across Australia and Asia. We offer a full range of environmental services to support successful and efficient project delivery from planning and approvals through to operations and closure with a strong understanding of regional legislation and regulatory compliance.


**Next Steps**


**If this opportunity sounds like a great fit, we’d love to hear from you!**


At AARC, we’re committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. Even if you don’t meet every requirement listed, we encourage you to apply – we value potential and are open to supporting your growth.


To apply, please submit your resume along with a brief cover letter that includes three words you’d use to describe yourself.


If you have any questions about the role or would like to learn more, feel free to reach out to our People \& Culture team at ausrecruitment@apemgroup.com.

jobs.title: Lecturer Pool - Department of Environmental Science, Policy, and Management
jobs.description:
Lecturer Pool - Department of Environmental Science, Policy, and Management - Rausser College of Natural Resources

Position Overview

Salary range:
The UC academic salary scales set the minimum pay at appointment. See the following table(s) for the current salary scale(s) for this position: https://www.ucop.edu/academic-personnel-programs/_files/2025-26/represented-july-2025-scales/t15.pdf. The current full-time salary range for this position is $70,977-$199,722.

Percent time:
Appointments are typically part-time.

Anticipated start:
Positions usually start in January, various times throughout the summer and August.

Review timeline:
Applications will be accepted and reviewed for any department needs through August 13, 2026. Applicants are considered for positions as needs arise; the existence of this applicant pool does not guarantee that a position is available. Applications are typically reviewed for fall course needs in April/May, spring course needs in October/November, and summer session course needs in January/February.

Position duration:
Varied and may be renewable based on need, funding, and performance.

Application Window

Open date:
June 4, 2025

Most recent review date:
Monday, Dec 1, 2025 at 11:59pm (Pacific Time)

Applications received after this date will be reviewed by the search committee if the position has not yet been filled.

Final date:
Thursday, Aug 13, 2026 at 11:59pm (Pacific Time)

Applications will continue to be accepted until this date, but those received after the review date will only be considered if the position has not yet been filled.

Position Description

The Department of Environmental Science, Policy, and Management (ESPM) in the Rausser College of Natural Resources at the University of California, Berkeley invites applications for a one-year pool of qualified temporary lecturers to teach courses with a focus area of Environmental Science, Policy, and Management. When a need arises, we seek lecturers who can teach/co-teach lecture, seminar, and/or laboratory courses in environmental science, policy, and management, both at the undergraduate and graduate levels.

ESPM is committed to creating an inclusive environment, one that is supportive of all individuals, regardless of background. The Department seeks candidates who can support the success of all students through inclusive curriculum, classroom environment, and pedagogy.

General Duties

In addition to teaching responsibilities, general duties include holding office hours, assigning grades, advising students, preparing course materials (e.g., syllabus), and maintaining a course website.

Labor Contract
: https://ucnet.universityofcalifornia.edu/labor/bargaining-units/ix/index.html

Qualifications

Basic qualifications
(required at time of application)

Advanced degree, or enrolled in an advanced degree program, at the time of application

Additional Qualifications (required At Time Of Start)

Advanced degree required by the start date of the position.
For those who are not US citizens or permanent residents, a legal permit that allows work in the United States (such as a US visa that allows employment) is required by the start date of the position. The department is unable to provide a visa/work permit.

Preferred Qualifications

The completion of a Ph.D. or equivalent international degree
Documented experience in teaching one of the biological sciences at the college level is strongly preferred.
Ability to support the success of all students through inclusive curriculum, classroom environment, and pedagogy

Application Requirements

Document requirements

Cover Letter/ Statement of Teaching - 1-2 page cover letter. Please discuss any prior teaching experience, teaching approach, and future teaching interests. This can include, for example, specific efforts, accomplishments, and future plans to support the success of all students through inclusive curriculum, classroom environment, and pedagogy.
Curriculum Vitae - Your most recently updated C.V.

Reference Requirements

3 required (contact information only)

Apply link:
https://aprecruit.berkeley.edu/JPF04961

Help contact:
nlowy@berkeley.edu

About UC Berkeley

UC Berkeley is committed to diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging in our public mission of research, teaching, and service, consistent with UC Regents Policy 4400 and University of California Academic Personnel policy (APM 210 1-d). These values are embedded in our Principles of Community, which reflect our passion for critical inquiry, debate, discovery and innovation, and our deep commitment to contributing to a better world. Every member of the UC Berkeley community has a role in sustaining a safe, caring and humane environment in which these values can thrive.

The University of California, Berkeley is an Equal Opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, age, or protected veteran status.

For more information, please refer to the University of California's Affirmative Action and Nondiscrimination in Employment Policy and the University of California's Anti-Discrimination Policy.

In searches when letters of reference are required all letters will be treated as confidential per University of California policy and California state law. Please refer potential referees, including when letters are provided via a third party (i.e., dossier service or career center), to the UC Berkeley statement of confidentiality prior to submitting their letter.

As a University employee, you will be required to comply with all applicable University policies and/or collective bargaining agreements, as may be amended from time to time. Federal, state, or local government directives may impose additional requirements.

Unless stated otherwise, unambiguously, in the position description, this position does not include sponsorship of a new consular H-1B visa petition that would require payment of the $100,000 supplemental fee.

As a condition of employment, the finalist will be required to disclose if they are subject to any final
administrative or judicial decisions within the last seven years determining that they committed any misconduct.

“Misconduct” means any violation of the policies or laws governing conduct at the applicant's previous place of employment, including, but not limited to, violations of policies or laws prohibiting sexual harassment, sexual assault, or other forms of harassment or discrimination, as defined by the employer.
UC Sexual Violence and Sexual Harassment Policy
UC Anti-Discrimination Policy
APM - 035: Affirmative Action and Nondiscrimination in Employment

Job location

Berkeley, CA

To apply, visit https://aprecruit.berkeley.edu/JPF04961
